evals.prompt: My company is a mid-size SaaS platform doing about $15M ARR in the HR tech space (employee engagement surveys). Our CEO wants to explore entering the Chinese market — we've had a few inbound inquiries from companies in Shenzhen and Shanghai. I need a strategy deck for the board meeting next month. Should we go direct, find a local partner, or acquire someone? We have no presence in APAC right now. Output as HTML slides please.
evals.expected_output: A structured China market entry strategy delivered as HTML slides, following the full 7-phase workflow with problem-scoped agents and Partner review.

assertions.text: Phase 0 checks file/web permissions and detects Agent Teams availability
Phase 0 recommends AkShare MCP for China data and Octagon MCP for competitive intel
Phase 1 restates the problem and asks clarifying questions (budget, timeline, target segment, regulatory concerns, board risk appetite)
Phase 1 confirms HTML slides as output format before proceeding
Phase 2 deploys problem-scoped agents (not data-scoped) — e.g., entry mode analysis, regulatory risk, comparable APAC entries
Partner Evaluator reviews synthesized findings before any user checkpoint
Phase 5 presents full storyline outline and requires user sign-off before deliverable
Does NOT read sub-skill files (frontend-slides) until Phase 6
Deliverable uses pyramid structure with charts and grouped citations
Phase 7 proposes specific next steps (e.g., partner interview questions, customer survey draft)
Saves engagement-state.yaml for resumability

evals.prompt: I'm the head of strategy at a regional grocery chain (about 120 stores across the US Midwest, ~$2B revenue). Walmart and Amazon Fresh are squeezing our margins and we're losing foot traffic in suburban locations. The board wants a 3-year plan to either differentiate or consider strategic alternatives (partnership, sale, pivot to e-commerce). I have our internal P&L data I can share. Give me a Word doc I can circulate to the leadership team.
evals.expected_output: A 3-year strategic plan delivered as .docx, incorporating user's internal P&L data, with competitive benchmarking against national grocers.

assertions.text: Phase 0 verifies docx skill availability and recommends Octagon + FRED MCPs
Phase 1 asks about declining metrics, affected markets, prior differentiation attempts, and requests the internal P&L data
Phase 1 confirms .docx output format
Phase 2 agents are problem-scoped (differentiation strategies, strategic alternatives, financial benchmarking) — not data-scoped
Partner Evaluator stress-tests comparability of differentiation cases and financial benchmarks
Incorporates user's internal P&L data into hypothesis validation when provided
Phase 5 presents 3-year plan outline with financial projections and risk matrix
Does NOT read docx sub-skill until Phase 6
Phase 7 proposes actionable follow-ups (customer surveys, competitor interviews) with draft materials

evals.prompt: We're a Series B fintech startup ($8M raised, 50 employees) building a cross-border payments platform focused on SMBs. Our investors are pushing us to figure out our unit economics and prove we can get to profitability by 2027. Right now our take rate is 0.8% but our CAC is high and we're not sure if our current customer segments are the right ones to focus on. Can you help me build a business plan that I can put in Notion for the team to collaborate on?
evals.expected_output: A unit economics and profitability business plan delivered to Notion, with segment analysis and competitive positioning.

assertions.text: Phase 0 checks Notion MCP is configured — blocks and guides setup if missing
Phase 0 recommends Octagon MCP for fintech competitive/funding data
Phase 1 asks about customer segments, geographic focus, transaction size, churn, LTV, CAC breakdown
Phase 2 agents are problem-scoped (unit economics optimization, profitability path, competitive positioning)
Partner Evaluator challenges unit economics assumptions and profitability timeline realism
Phase 5 presents segment strategy, unit economics model, and profitability roadmap for sign-off
Notion output includes collaborative sections for team input
Phase 7 proposes specific follow-ups (customer interviews, A/B test designs) with draft materials
